rationale: Brain & Cognitive merges Neuroscience and Psychology at level 1, so level 2 needs to give both communities distinct homes without re-creating the level-1 split. Fourteen subspecialties split by approach (systems neuro vs. cognitive psych vs. clinical psych) rather than by topic. I kept Computational Neuroscience separate from Systems Neuroscience because the methods and journals genuinely diverge (NIPS-flavored vs. Cell-Neuron-flavored). Affective Science gets its own card now — emotion research used to be folded into clinical psych but the past decade made it a distinct field with its own journals and labs.

subspecialties.name: Systems Neuroscience
subspecialties.tagline: Circuits, recordings, optogenetics, behavior in animals.
covers: circuit-level neuroscience, electrophysiology, optogenetics, calcium imaging, animal behavior, sensorimotor systems
Neuron, Nat Neurosci

subspecialties.name: Cognitive Neuroscience
subspecialties.tagline: Human brain imaging, attention, memory, decision.
covers: fMRI, EEG/MEG human studies, attention, memory, decision making, social cognition neuroimaging
J Cog Neurosci, NeuroImage
explicitly_excludes: pure behavior without imaging → Cognitive Psychology

subspecialties.name: Computational Neuroscience
subspecialties.tagline: Models of neurons, networks, learning rules.
covers: spiking network models, Bayesian brain, neural coding theory, learning rules, theoretical neuroscience
PLoS Comp Biol, J Comp Neurosci
explicitly_excludes: deep learning methodology → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Cognitive Psychology
covers: attention, working memory, perception, judgment and decision making, language comprehension as behavior
Psych Sci, JEP series

subspecialties.name: Developmental Psychology
subspecialties.tagline: Infant, child, adolescent cognition and behavior.
covers: infant cognition, child development, language acquisition, social-emotional development, adolescence research
Child Dev, Dev Sci

subspecialties.name: Social Psychology
covers: attitudes, social cognition, intergroup relations, prejudice, persuasion, prosocial behavior, behavior change
JPSP, Pers Soc Psychol Bull

subspecialties.name: Clinical Psychology
covers: psychotherapy outcomes (CBT, ACT, DBT), psychopathology, clinical assessment, psychometrics for mental health
J Consult Clin Psychol, Clin Psychol Sci

subspecialties.name: Affective Science
covers: emotion theory and measurement, mood, emotion regulation, interoception, affective dynamics
Emotion, Affect Sci

subspecialties.name: Neuropsychology & Cognitive Disorders
covers: neuropsych assessment, traumatic brain injury research, dementia cognition, lesion-deficit studies
J Int Neuropsychol Soc, Neuropsychol Rev

subspecialties.name: Addiction & Reward Science
subspecialties.tagline: Reward circuits, decision under risk, substance use neuro.
covers: reward and motivation circuits, substance use neuroscience, decision making under risk, gambling research
Addiction Biology, Neuropsychopharmacology

subspecialties.name: Sleep & Circadian Science
covers: sleep architecture research, circadian biology, chronobiology, sleep-memory interactions
Sleep, J Sleep Res

subspecialties.name: Psycholinguistics & Language Science
covers: sentence processing, bilingualism, reading, prediction in language, neural basis of language
Cognition, Lang Cogn Neurosci

subspecialties.name: Quantitative Methods & Replication Science
subspecialties.tagline: Statistics for psychology, replication, open science.
covers: psychometrics, replication research, meta-science, registered reports methodology, multilevel modeling for psych
Adv Methods Pract Psychol Sci, Psychol Methods

subspecialties.name: Comparative & Evolutionary Cognition
subspecialties.tagline: Animal cognition, primate research, evolution of mind.
covers: primate cognition, bird cognition, comparative neuroanatomy of cognition, evolution of social intelligence
Anim Cogn, Trends Cogn Sci comparative

uncovered_concerns: Considered Educational Psychology — folds under Cognitive Psychology (learning) or Social (development); the level-1 Social Sciences has Education Research already.
Considered Industrial-Organizational Psychology — small (~1% of papers), folds under Social Sciences (Business & Management overlap).
Considered separating Memory from Attention — both fit cleanly under Cognitive Psychology or Cognitive Neuroscience depending on method.
